references.title: Student achievement prediction using deep neural network from multi-source campus data

extracted.Data sources: Web Browsing, Consumption Data, Library, Gateway Logins, Student Demographics
extracted.Features: [Sequences of Logs, OHE vectors of static data]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
For target, the authors discretize GPA into a multiclass problem where the top X% (X=5, 10, 15, 20) are Excellent, the bottom X% are poor and all students in the middle become considered as good. 

For every feature, authors select different levels of aggregation - 4 hours for web page browsing. In others, aggregation was daily.

Records with same data, time and place were merged.

Static data is converted into a OHE vector .

Depending on the source, data is either interpreted by an LSTM module or a 2DCNN.

Class imbalance addressed with weighted loss function instead of the common cross-entropy.

A way to augment the data was to divide the chunks of 145 days from the sample into 10 pieces and consider each piece as a new sample → with the same label as the original student.
extracted.Models: Logistic Regression, CART, AdaBoost, Random Forest, DO. V, Do.T,
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model:
20% threshold

{Accuracy: 0.83 - Do. T, Macro Precision: 0.85- Do. T, Macro Recall: 0.78 - Do. T, Macro F1-Score: 0.80 - Do. T}
extracted.Comments:
Authors use a different approach than most other works. In this case, they use campus data - card data essentially, to predict GPA. Logs are not only LMS logs, but rather logs collected from the entire institution. 

Results shown only consider the target definition as having 20% of the data. Reason being that the limits between classes are not as strict.
extracted.Limitations:
Oversampling via chunking a sequence would only be ideal if the behaviour every 2 weeks was consistent. 

Holdout Method

Hard to Replicate for other institutions

Does not consider data related with learner interactions with course materials

annotations.text: It is essencial to make early identification of struggling students and many models use traditional methods with features built from domain knowledge.

Paper presents end to end model that makes feature extraction from multi souce behavioral data to predict performance. Approach combines LSTM and CNN and tests with data from the University of Beijing show that the DL model outperforms multiple ML conventional ML models.
